summary.title: OpenArchive local-first architecture direction
summary.body_text: OpenArchive slice 1 is moving to a fully local Docker Compose deployment with Postgres, enrichment, query services, and optional Ollama. Binary payload storage moves out of the database into local volume-backed object storage behind an abstraction, with future S3 support planned. MCP is the primary external interface, while the core stays provider- and protocol-agnostic and prefers synchronous Rust with a database-backed queue.

classifications.title: Define local-first slice-one direction
classifications.body_text: The artifact sets concrete implementation direction for deployment, storage boundaries, interface shape, and concurrency posture.

memories.title: Slice 1 uses Docker Compose locally
memories.body_text: Slice 1 is planned as a local Docker Compose deployment with containerized Postgres, enrichment, query, and possibly Ollama.

memories.title: Blob storage moves out of the database
memories.body_text: Raw artifact blobs move to local volume-backed object storage through an abstraction, with future S3 bucket support planned.

memories.title: MCP is primary but the core stays transport-agnostic
memories.body_text: Local MCP is the primary external interface for slice 1, but the application core should not become MCP-protocol-shaped.

memories.title: Oracle is only a provider
memories.body_text: Oracle should be treated as just another provider like Postgres behind config-driven factories.

memories.title: Prefer sync Rust and preserve lineage
memories.body_text: The project prefers sync Rust, uses a database-backed queue, and wants edited files to create new artifacts so lineage is preserved.
